description: Agent creates a custom function and wires it so the AEM visual rule editor can find it. The agent must: (1) set customFunctionsPath in form.json pointing to the JS file, (2) use the function-declaration + export {} pattern (not inline export function), and (3) include a JSDoc @name tag. Regression test for the functions.js entry point documentation fix.
userMessage: Add a custom function `calculateAge` to this form. It should take an ISO date-of-birth string and return the person's age in whole years. Make sure it appears in the AEM visual rule editor so it can be used in rules.

rubric.description: The agent sets `customFunctionsPath` in form.json pointing to the JS file it created. This is the sole runtime entry point AEM uses to load custom functions — without it, the function will never appear in the rule editor.

The function is written as a regular `function calculateAge(...) {}` declaration followed by `export { calculateAge }` at the end of the file. The agent does NOT use `export function calculateAge` (inline export), which the AEM parser does not recognise.
The function has a JSDoc comment with a `@name` tag (e.g. `@name calculateAge Calculate Age`). Without @name, the function does not appear in the visual rule editor.
If the function accepts a globals/scope parameter, it is the last argument and annotated with `@param {scope} globals`. Pure utility functions that take no globals parameter are exempt.
